pi_items.source: O*NET OnLine (https://www.onetonline.org/link/summary/13-1041.01) confirms the official title is plural 'Environmental Compliance Inspectors'. The singular form 'Environmental Compliance Inspector' is an accepted alternate job title for this occupation.

pi_items.verified_value: Environmental Consulting Services (NAICS 541620) is a valid but non-primary industry. O*NET lists Government and Finance/Insurance as the top employing industries for 13-1041.01.
pi_items.source: O*NET OnLine (https://www.onetonline.org/link/summary/13-1041.01) top industries are Government and Finance/Insurance. NAICS 541620 verified via https://www.naics.com/naics-code-description/?code=541620 and IBISWorld. Job postings confirm environmental compliance inspectors work at consulting firms (e.g., Accura Engineering & Consulting Services on Lensa.com).
O*NET OnLine (https://www.onetonline.org/link/summary/13-1041.01) confirms 13-1041.01 = Environmental Compliance Inspectors, a specialty of 13-1041.00 Compliance Officers.

Web search found 'Pacific Northwest Environmental LLC' (pnwenvironmental.com) — a real company doing asbestos abatement and demolition, NOT environmental compliance consulting. No entity named 'Pacific Northwest Environmental Services, Inc.' found in public records, Bloomberg, or WA State L&I databases. The company is fictional, created for this task.

privileged_info_summary: The source document 'environmental_compliance_report_raw.docx' is entirely synthetic, generated by a Python script (create_docx_files.py) for a fictional company 'Pacific Northwest Environmental Services, Inc.' in Tacoma, WA. The document is approximately 8-9 pages (1,748 words) with all text in Normal style — no heading styles, section breaks, page numbers, headers, footers, captions, or cross-references are pre-applied. The document contains exactly 7 Heading 1 targets: 'Executive Summary', '1. Introduction', '2. Air Quality Monitoring', '3. Water Quality and Stormwater Management', '4. Waste Management', '5. Compliance Summary and Corrective Actions', and 'Appendices'. It contains exactly 17 Heading 2 targets: 1.1 Purpose and Scope, 1.2 Regulatory Framework, 1.3 Facility Description, 2.1 Emission Sources and Controls, 2.2 Ambient Monitoring Results, 2.3 Compliance Status, 3.1 Discharge Monitoring, 3.2 Effluent Monitoring Data, 3.3 Stormwater BMP Effectiveness, 4.1 Hazardous Waste Generation, 4.2 Waste Minimization Initiatives, 4.3 Storage and Disposal Compliance, 5.1 Notices of Violation, 5.2 Corrective Actions Summary, 5.3 Planned Improvements for 2025, Appendix A: Laboratory Analytical Methods, and Appendix B: Monitoring Equipment Calibration Records. The document contains exactly 3 Word tables: Table 1 has 13 rows and 3 columns (Month, PM2.5 ug/m3, NOx ppb — 12 months of air quality data), Table 2 has 5 rows and 5 columns (Quarter, BOD5 mg/L, TSS mg/L, pH, Oil & Grease mg/L — 4 quarters of effluent data), and Table 3 has 7 rows and 4 columns (Waste Stream, RCRA Code, Quantity tons, Disposal Method — 6 hazardous waste streams). There are exactly 3 inline PNG images serving as figure placeholders (gray rectangles, 480x50 pixels each). There are exactly 6 cross-reference text placeholders in the body: '[see Table 1]' in section 2.2, '[see Figure 1]' in section 2.3, '[see Table 2]' in section 3.2, '[see Figure 2]' in section 3.3, '[see Table 3]' in section 4.1, and '[see Figure 3]' in section 4.3. The task requires 3 section breaks creating regions: Title Page (no page number), Front Matter with Roman numeral footers, and Body with Arabic footers starting at 1. The agent must also insert a Table of Contents after the Executive Summary, add Word auto-numbered captions (SEQ fields) to all 6 objects, replace all 6 bracketed placeholders with live cross-references (REF fields), and insert a Table of Figures (TOC field with \c flag). The output must be saved as 'environmental_compliance_final.docx' in Desktop\WordTasks. The environment runs Microsoft Word 2010 (Office 14) on Windows 11 via QEMU. The References tab in Word 2010 provides Insert Caption, Cross-reference, Table of Contents, and Insert Table of Figures — all confirmed accessible during live testing. Section breaks are inserted via Page Layout > Breaks > Next Page. Per-section page numbering requires unlinking 'Link to Previous' in the footer before changing the format.
